lion.name: Lion
lion.description: The lion is a large cat of the genus Panthera native to Africa and India. It is the second-largest living cat after the tiger.
lion.habitat: Grasslands, savannas, dense scrub, and open woodlands

tiger.name: Tiger
tiger.description: The tiger is the largest living cat species and a member of the genus Panthera. It is known for its distinctive orange coat with black stripes.
tiger.habitat: Tropical forests, grasslands, and mangrove swamps

elephant.name: Elephant
elephant.description: Elephants are the largest existing land animals. They are known for their long trunk, tusks, large ear flaps, and massive legs.

bald_eagle.name: Bald Eagle
bald_eagle.description: The bald eagle is a bird of prey found in North America. It is the national bird of the United States.
bald_eagle.habitat: Near large bodies of open water with abundant fish

cardinal.name: Northern Cardinal
cardinal.description: The northern cardinal is a mid-sized songbird with a distinctive crest on the head and a mask on the face.

blue_jay.name: Blue Jay
blue_jay.description: The blue jay is a passerine bird in the family Corvidae, native to eastern North America.
blue_jay.habitat: Deciduous and mixed forests, parks, and suburban areas

owl.name: Great Horned Owl
owl.description: The great horned owl is a large owl native to the Americas. It is known for its distinctive ear tufts and deep hooting call.

sparrow.name: House Sparrow
sparrow.description: The house sparrow is a small bird found throughout the world. It is strongly associated with human habitations.
sparrow.habitat: Urban areas, farms, and suburban environments

wolf.name: Gray Wolf
wolf.description: The gray wolf is a canine native to the wilderness and remote areas of Eurasia and North America.

bear.name: Brown Bear
bear.description: The brown bear is a large bear species found across North America and Eurasia. It includes the grizzly bear subspecies.
